comment: E13 signed profile bundle example (docs/mcp-signed-profile-bundles.md). Wraps the E09 profiles from examples/mcp/tool-profile.example.json in the signed distribution envelope: issuer + audience + validity window + upstream-namespace ceiling + revocation pointer + mandatory detached signature. The signature value below is a PLACEHOLDER (base64 of 64 zero bytes, clearly fake) — E13 is design-only and nothing verifies it; E14 enforcement would refuse this bundle with bundle_signature_invalid (-32015).

issuer.comment: Trust decisions use key_id plus the consumer's local trusted-keys file only; display is for audit rows and refusal messages.

dev-default.comment: Team baseline and ceiling: sees every tool on both local upstreams and may call everything it sees. Every rule is covered by allowed_upstream_namespaces — a rule naming any other upstream would be a static load error (bundle_audience_mismatch, -32018).

reviewer.comment: Read-only reviewer: inherits the dev-default ceiling, keeps the same visibility, but may call only two read tools. extends resolves only inside this bundle — bundles are self-contained, no cross-file parents in either direction.

ci-minimal.comment: CI runtime profile, typically selected via UNLIMITED_SKILLS_MCP_PROFILE on the 'host:ci-runner' audience: inherits the reviewer ceiling and narrows to a single visible, callable tool.

revocation.comment: Local CRL file (design-only in v1): revoked bundle SHA-256s and revoked key_ids. Declared-but-unreadable is fail-closed bundle_revoked (-32017). registry_endpoint is omitted here; when present it is carried for the future registry gate and never fetched in v1.

signature.comment: PLACEHOLDER signature: base64 of 64 zero bytes — obviously fake, for shape illustration only. A real value is the detached Ed25519 signature over the canonical JSON (UTF-8, sorted keys, no insignificant whitespace) of this document with the 'signature' member removed.
